writing_exemplar.carried_over: Introduction arc from a familiar causal mediation target to sharp bounds under one explicitly named violation, adapted here to a clean mediator plus a bounded direct leak.

locked_parameters.observable_distribution: P over (X,M,Y) with X\in\{0,1\}, M\in\{0,1\}, Y\in[0,1], summarized by p_x=\Pr(X=x), q_{m\mid x}=\Pr(M=m\mid X=x), r_{x,m}=\E[Y\mid X=x,M=m].
locked_parameters.parameter_theta: \theta=\mathrm{ACE}=\E[Y(\mathrm{do}\,X=1)]-\E[Y(\mathrm{do}\,X=0)] in the graph \mathcal G with edges U\to X, U\to Y, X\to M, M\to Y, X\to Y.
locked_parameters.identifying_restrictions: U has finite support with |\mathcal U|\le K:=|X|\,|M|+1=5 (Caratheodory reduction); the mediator is clean, U\perp M\mid X; the structural mean \varphi(m,x,u)=\E[Y\mid M=m,X=x,U=u]\in[0,1]; the latent controlled direct effect d(m,u)=\varphi(m,1,u)-\varphi(m,0,u) obeys |d(m,u)|\le\lambda.
locked_parameters.bounding_functional: I(\lambda)=\{\,\mathrm{ACE}:\exists\,(\pi,w_0,w_1,\varphi)\text{ feasible}\,\} with endpoint map \lambda\mapsto[L(\lambda),U(\lambda)], where feasibility means the four clean-mediator compatibility equations r_{x,m}=\sum_u\varphi(m,x,u)w_x(u), the Bayes consistency \pi=\sum_x p_x w_x, the box \varphi\in[0,1], and |d|\le\lambda.

named_assumptions.label: ass:finite
named_assumptions.statement: The latent confounder U has finite support \mathcal U with |\mathcal U|\le K:=|X|\,|M|+1=5. All optimization and feasibility statements are taken over this finite latent support.

named_assumptions.label: ass:clean
named_assumptions.statement: The mediator is clean in the front-door sense: U\perp M\mid X. Equivalently, \Pr(U\in\cdot\mid X,M)=\Pr(U\in\cdot\mid X), so the four compatibility equations \eqref{eq:compat} hold.

named_assumptions.label: ass:bounded
named_assumptions.statement: The observed outcome satisfies Y\in[0,1]. Therefore every structural mean obeys 0\le \varphi(m,x,u)\le 1 for all (m,x,u).

named_assumptions.label: ass:leak
named_assumptions.statement: There is a known sensitivity radius \lambda\ge 0 such that |\varphi(m,1,u)-\varphi(m,0,u)|\le \lambda for every (m,u)\in\{0,1\}\times\mathcal U. The corner \lambda=0 is the exclusion restriction with no direct X\to Y path.

named_assumptions.label: ass:overlap
named_assumptions.statement: Both treatment arms occur with positive probability, p_x>0 for x\in\{0,1\}, and both mediator states occur within each treatment arm, q_{m\mid x}>0 for every (x,m)\in\{0,1\}^2. Hence each conditional mean r_{x,m} is well defined on observed support.

conjectures.label: nondecomp
conjectures.statement:
extnormal{(NOT YET PROVED)} Let $\kappa(P)$ be the observable signature \eqref{eq:kappa}.
 egin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*),leftmargin=2em,itemsep=1pt]
  \item For all $\lambda\ge0$, $[L(\lambda),U(\lambda)]\subseteq B_{\mathrm{box}}(\lambda)\cap B_{\mathrm{sens}}(\lambda)$.
  \item On the relatively open observable set $\{P:\kappa(P)\neq0\}$, there is $\lambda_0(P)>0$ such that
        for $0<\lambda<\lambda_0(P)$ the inclusion in (a) is \emph{strict} in both factors.
  \item At the exclusion corner the per-mediator box closes:
        $B_{\mathrm{box}}(0)=I(0)=\{\theta_{\mathrm{FD}}\}$, so the strict inclusion in (b) against
        $B_{\mathrm{box}}$ is necessarily a strictly-positive-$\lambda$ phenomenon. The compatibility-free
        interval is loose already at the corner, $I(0)\subsetneq B_{\mathrm{sens}}(0)$, because
        $B_{\mathrm{sens}}$ discards \eqref{eq:compat}.
\end{enumerate}

method_exemplar.carried_over: Recover the front-door functional exactly at \lambda=0 and then measure how the shared latent-completion constraints perturb that corner when the direct path is allowed to leak.

conjectures.label: vertex
conjectures.statement:
Is there a finite family of rational functions $\{v_j(\lambda)\}_{j\in J}$ of $(p_x,q_{m\mid x},r_{x,m},\lambda)$,
indexed by active-set cells of the lifted polytope of \Sef{sec:setup}, with
\[
  L(\lambda)=\min_{j\in J}v_j(\lambda),\qquad U(\lambda)=\max_{j\in J}v_j(\lambda),
\]
and a closed-form threshold $\lambda^\star=\lambda^\star(P)>0$ at which the maximizing (resp.\ minimizing)
cell changes, so that $U(\cdot)$ (resp.\ $L(\cdot)$) is piecewise-rational with a kink at $\lambda^\star$?

\smallskip
oindent	extbf{No-kink convention.} Set
$\lambda^\star(P):=\inf\{\lambda>0:	ext{the maximizing cell at }\lambda	ext{ differs from the cell active at }0^+\}$,
with $\lambda^\star(P):=+\infty$ when no such switch occurs---a single cell is maximizing throughout, as on the
degenerate support $|\mathcal U|=1$ (Corollary~ef{cor:degenerate}) or wherever one branch dominates for all
$\lambda$. The kink claim is scoped to the generic regime $\{P:0<\lambda^\star(P)<\infty\}$; on its complement
$U(\cdot)$ is a single rational branch with no interior kink and the construction returns that branch. The
symmetric convention defines the minimizing-cell threshold for $L(\cdot)$.

\smallskip
oindent	extbf{Handle:} lift the bilinear program with $g_{m,x,u}=\varphi(m,x,u)w_x(u)$ so the
compatibility equations \eqref{eq:compat} become linear; for fixed $(w_0,w_1)$ on the latent simplices,
enumerate the active-set cells of \Sef{sec:setup} (which box bounds $g_{m,x,u}\in\{0,w_x(u)\}$ and which
leak bounds $|d(m,u)|\le\lambda$ bind), read each cell's vertex value of \eqref{eq:ace} as a rational
function $v_j$ of the data and $(w_0,w_1)$, optimize over $(w_0,w_1)$, and locate $\lambda^\star$ as the
smallest $\lambda$ solving the boundary equality $v_{j}(\lambda)=v_{j'}(\lambda)$ between the two adjacent
maximizing cells. Start from $|\mathcal U|=2$, the smallest support allowing $w_0
eq w_1$.
	extbf{Why it matters:} a closed-form vertex formula lets front-door software report the leak interval and
its kink directly from $(p_x,q_{m\mid x},r_{x,m})$ without solving a nonconvex program at every $\lambda$.

method_exemplar.carried_over: Carry over finite latent-type enumeration and vertex endpoint geometry, modified from an LP to the present lifted bilinear shared-U program.

secondary_method_exemplars.carried_over: Encode discrete latent and conditional-independence restrictions as finite optimization constraints before optimizing the causal target.

conjectures.label: inference
conjectures.statement:
Is there an estimator of $ igl(L(\lambda),U(\lambda) igr)$ that is $\sqrt n$-consistent and asymptotically
Gaussian for fixed $\lambda$ in the interior of an active regime, and whose limit at the kink $\lambda^\star$
is the (non-Gaussian) directional law? Here $\lambda^\star=\lambda^\star(P)$ is the threshold of Open-ended
Question~ef{oeq:vertex} under its no-kink convention; on $\{P:\lambda^\star(P)=+\infty\}$ the kink regime is
empty and only the interior Gaussian limit is in force.

\smallskip
oindent	extbf{Handle:} cross-fit the nuisances $(\hat p_x,\hat q_{m\mid x},\hat r_{x,m})$ on
held-out folds and plug them into the Open-ended Question~ef{oeq:vertex} vertex map
$v_j(\cdot)$, then apply the directional delta method of \citet{FangSantos2019DirectionallyDifferentiable} to
the $\min/\max$ envelope; the active-set indicator at $\lambda^\star$ supplies the directional derivative
along the binding face.
	extbf{Case-split fallback:} if no single procedure is valid across regimes, deliver one per regime---(a)
interior active set: regular $\sqrt n$ Gaussian inference on the selected vertex; (b) kink
$\lambda=\lambda^\star$: a directional limit with a rescaled-bootstrap or numerical-derivative correction; (c)
collapse $\lambda=0$: the degenerate point limit at $	heta_{\mathrm{FD}}$, coordinated with set-level
coverage in the sense of \citet{ChernozhukovHongTamer2007ParameterSets}.
	extbf{Why it matters:} it turns the leak-indexed interval into a reportable confidence band over
$\lambda$ for empirical mediation studies, the inference object \citet{ChernozhukovHongTamer2007ParameterSets}
calls for in set-identified models.

method_exemplar.carried_over: Apply the directional delta method to the min/max envelope of the finite-vertex endpoint map at active-face kink regimes.

secondary_method_exemplars.carried_over: Transfer pointwise endpoint limits into valid set-level confidence statements for an interval-valued causal target.

supporting_theorems.label: collapse
supporting_theorems.statement: At \lambda=0 the set I(0) is the singleton \[ \theta_{\mathrm{FD}}=\sum_{m}\bigl(q_{m\mid1}-q_{m\mid0}\bigr)\bigl(p_1 r_{1,m}+p_0 r_{0,m}\bigr), \] Pearl's front-door functional.
supporting_theorems.proof_tex:
egin{theorem}[Supporting theorem S1: exclusion collapse]\label{thm:collapse}
At \(\lambda=0\) the set \(I(0)\) is the singleton
\[
  	heta_{\mathrm{FD}}=\sum_{m} igl(q_{m\mid1}-q_{m\mid0} igr) igl(p_1 r_{1,m}+p_0 r_{0,m} igr),
\]
Pearl's front-door functional.
\end{theorem}
 egin{proof}
Fix any feasible completion at \(\lambda=0\). The leak restriction gives \(\varphi(m,1,u)=\varphi(m,0,u)=: \varphi(m,u)\) for every \((m,u)\). Substituting into the causal functional yields
\[
  	heta=\sum_m igl(q_{m\mid1}-q_{m\mid0} igr)\sum_u \varphi(m,u)\pi(u).
\]
By Bayes consistency, \(\pi(u)=p_0 w_0(u)+p_1 w_1(u)\), so for each mediator value \(m\),
\[
  \sum_u \varphi(m,u)\pi(u)=p_0\sum_u \varphi(m,u)w_0(u)+p_1\sum_u \varphi(m,u)w_1(u).
\]
Because \(\lambda=0\) removes the treatment index from \(\varphi\), the compatibility equations become
\(r_{x,m}=\sum_u \varphi(m,u)w_x(u)\). Hence
\[
  \sum_u \varphi(m,u)\pi(u)=p_0 r_{0,m}+p_1 r_{1,m}.
\]
Substituting back gives
\[
  	heta=\sum_m igl(q_{m\mid1}-q_{m\mid0} igr) igl(p_1 r_{1,m}+p_0 r_{0,m} igr)=	heta_{\mathrm{FD}}.
\]
Thus every feasible completion at \(\lambda=0\) produces the same causal value. It remains to show that the feasible set is nonempty. Take a two-point latent support \(\mathcal U=\{u_1,u_2\}\), embedded in the allowed five-point support by assigning zero mass to the remaining points. Define \(w_1(u_1)=1\), \(w_0(u_2)=1\), \(\pi(u_1)=p_1\), \(\pi(u_2)=p_0\), and for each mediator value \(m\), set
\[
  \varphi(m,0,u_1)=\varphi(m,1,u_1)=r_{1,m},\qquad\varphi(m,0,u_2)=\varphi(m,1,u_2)=r_{0,m}.
\]
These choices satisfy \(0\le \varphi\le 1\), the leak restriction with \(\lambda=0\), Bayes consistency, and the four compatibility equations. Therefore \(I(0)\) is nonempty and every element equals \(	heta_{\mathrm{FD}}\), so \(I(0)=\{	heta_{\mathrm{FD}}\}\).
\end{proof}

supporting_theorems.label: interval
supporting_theorems.statement: For every \lambda\ge0, I(\lambda) is nonempty and compact; writing L(\lambda)=\min I(\lambda) and U(\lambda)=\max I(\lambda), the map \lambda\mapsto L(\lambda) is nonincreasing, \lambda\mapsto U(\lambda) is nondecreasing, and I(\lambda)\subseteq I(\lambda') whenever \lambda\le\lambda' in the sense [L(\lambda),U(\lambda)]\subseteq[L(\lambda'),U(\lambda')]. Moreover L(0)=U(0)=\theta_{\mathrm{FD}}.

substrate_audit.reason: The survey confirms that the repository already has a finite-support partial-identification system abstraction, but the present note is a front-door latent-completion problem rather than a Manski IV system.

The survey confirms existing endpoint-style partial-identification bookkeeping, but the current endpoint map is driven by clean-mediator compatibility and a direct-leak constraint rather than by Manski worst-case completion.

No local declaration surfaced under that exact name in the surveyed root, and the theorem note works directly at the finite bilinear-program level instead of depending on an existing in-repo Balke-Pearl abstraction.

file_plan.reason: The present setup reuses the finite-support partial-identification perspective only at the analogy level; it does not instantiate an IV/Manski observable-law system.
The front-door leak interval is a different observable functional from the no-assumptions Manski ATE bounds, so no direct theorem-level reuse is appropriate in this Stage 0.0 artifact.
The motivating vertex geometry is Balke-Pearl-like, but the actual problem here is a lifted bilinear shared-U optimization rather than the existing linear IV bound template.
